mnemonics: F.R.I. Radio Waves - Frequencies Radiate Information
keyPoints: Unguided media transmits data through the air or vacuum without a physical path.
Common types include radio waves, microwaves, and infrared waves.
Signal propagation can be affected by obstacles, interference, and environmental conditions.
commonMistakes: Confusing unguided media (wireless) with guided media (wired) like twisted pair or fiber optic cables.
Assuming all wireless signals travel the same way, not recognizing the different propagation behaviors of radio waves vs. microwaves.
